route.name: Vapor Trail
route.description: Public GPX high-alpine Colorado loop with Colorado Trail singletrack, dirt roads, paved connectors, and big pass riding.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX high-alpine Colorado loop with Colorado Trail singletrack, dirt roads, paved connectors, and big pass riding.
route.bestSeason: July to October after snowmelt; verify current snow, monsoon storms, smoke, and high-elevation exposure.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX high-alpine Colorado loop with Colorado Trail singletrack, dirt roads, paved connectors, and big pass riding.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Many streams and springs, but all sources need filtering and final spacing from the GPX.
resources.food: Minor resupply at St. Elmo and Monarch Pass; start/finish services in Salida.
resources.camping: Established camps and dispersed options exist along the route; exact July 10-13 camps must be selected and verified.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before ride-ready promotion.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Treat as a fit-rider long-weekend candidate.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control ride-ready promotion.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: reject/rework for ride_ready; exact legal camps, high-country condition proof, and Stage 2 fire/weather refresh are missing
blockers: Not ride-ready until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
No exact legal overnight plan with land-manager proof.
No current snow/rideability proof for Tomichi/Monarch Crest high country.
Active Stage 2 fire restrictions on relevant forests; stove-only/no campfire plan must be explicit.
No final July 9-10 weather, smoke, lightning, and closure refresh.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh for next-weekend ride readiness.
Create campsite coordinates for each night and verify each against USFS MVUM/closure/camping restrictions.
Check PSICC and GMUG alerts on July 9 or July 10.
Check COTREX/Colorado Trail/Monarch Crest/local shop reports for snow, downed trees, and trail status.
Refresh NWS point forecasts for Salida, Monarch Pass, Tomichi Pass/Alpine Tunnel, and Monarch Crest within 24-48 hours.
Refresh smoke/fire via official fire dashboards/InciWeb immediately before departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Bikepacking lists July-October as normal season, but the route tops out above 12,500 ft and requires current snow/condition proof before July 10-13.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.finalDecisionNote: Source-backed route remains planning-grade; target-window blockers prevent ride-ready promotion only.
